channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Dozens of costumed canines get their chance to trick-or-treating at Howloween event in Michigan
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Dogs dressed up as everything from Elvis Presley to Scooby Doo’s Mystery Machine went trick-or-treating in Michigan’s capital Friday as part of the annual Howloween event organized by a local pet store.
item.dc:creator: MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he has commuted sentence of former US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday he had commuted the sentence of former U.S. Rep. George Santos, who is serving more than seven years in federal prison after pleading guilty to fraud and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Five cities hit with federal presence remain on edge
item.description: (NewsNation) — President Donald Trump has touted the success of his deployment of the National Guard and other federal agents to crack down on immigration and crime, but residents in these cities say this presence has damaged their sense of safety.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Philadelphia officials seek tips about Kada Scott, who disappeared 2 weeks ago
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Authorities in Philadelphia said Friday that they are no closer to locating a missing 23-year-old woman who disappeared from her nursing home job nearly two weeks ago, urging residents to come forward with even the smallest of potential clues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump-Zelensky meeting
item.description: President Trump hosted Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday at the White House, part of a renewed push to end Russia’s over three-years long war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Alaska storm damage so bad many evacuees won’t go home for at least 18 months, governor says
item.description: Damage to remote Alaska villages hammered by flooding last weekend is so extreme that many of the more than 2,000 people displaced won’t be able to return to their homes for at least 18 months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Trump official John Bolton pleads not guilty in Maryland court
item.description: A former top advisor to President Trump pleaded not guilty in a Maryland court Friday to mishandling classified information.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: From bills to berets: Notable moments from NYC's contentious mayoral debate
item.description: New York City’s mayoral candidates have duked it out in their first general election debate, with contenders Zohran Mamdani, Andrew Cuomo and Curtis Sliwa sparring over everything from the Israel-Hamas war to the city’s education system.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: LA county reaches tentative $828M agreement to settle 400 more claims of sexual abuse
item.description: Los Angeles County has reached a tentative $828 million agreement to settle more than 400 additional sexual abuse claims against county employees that were not included in an earlier settlement that was the largest sexual abuse settlement in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The other Alaska airlift: Volunteers save dogs from a flooded Alaska village, 1 tiny plane at a time
item.description: A nonprofit in rural Alaska is working to rescue dogs from Kipnuk, a village hit hard by Typhoon Halong.
item.dc:creator: Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Prince Andrew to give up royal title amid Epstein controversy
item.description: Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: With no takers yet, White House meets with colleges still weighing an agreement with Trump
item.description: Five remaining universities have been weighing an offer from President Donald Trump to sign a “compact” for higher education.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Father pleads guilty to murder of missing 7-month-old son in Southern California
item.description: The father of a 7-month-old boy missing in Southern California pleaded guilty to second-degree murder of his son after a monthslong investigation that has failed to locate the child's remains.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: An Arkansas father accused of murder says he's running for sheriff while awaiting trial
item.description: An Arkansas father accused of killing his daughter's alleged abuser is running for sheriff while awaiting his murder trial, saying he has seen the failures of law enforcement in his rural county.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sex is a big market for the AI industry. ChatGPT won't be the first to try to profit from it
item.description: ChatGPT will be able to have kinkier conversations after OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced the artificial intelligence company will soon allow its chatbot to engage in “erotica for verified adults." OpenAI won't be the first to try to profit from sexualized AI.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: How to watch the Orionid meteor shower, debris of Halley's comet
item.description: The Orionids — one of two major meteor showers caused by remnants from Halley’s comet — will peak with the arrival of a new moon, providing an excellent opportunity to see shooting stars without interference from moonlight.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON AP Science Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Disapproval of Trump rises in new Emerson College Polling survey: Political Power Ranker
item.description: President Donald Trump's disapproval rating continued to rise this month in the latest Emerson College Polling survey.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, WGN 9

item.title: John Bolton surrenders at Maryland courthouse
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. — John Bolton, the former national security adviser-turned-foe of President Trump, surrendered himself to authorities Friday morning after he was charged with unlawfully sharing and storing classified information. Bolton arrived at the federal courthouse in Greenbelt, Md.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP senators worry about Trump, Hegseth shutdown moves
item.description: Republican senators have questions and concerns about how President Donald Trump is managing to pay more than 1 million military service members during the government shutdown and are seeking more information about what funds he is dipping into to achieve his political ends.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Development is possible for tropical wave in Atlantic: NHC
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA)— Development is possible for a tropical wave in the central tropical Atlantic, the National Hurricane Center said. A tropical wave located east of the Windward Islands into the Caribbean Sea is associated with a large area of showers and thunderstorms.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Many swings, few knockout hits as Cuomo and Mamdani trade jabs in heated first NYC mayoral debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo entered Thursday evening’s first New York mayoral debate trying to blunt Democratic front-runner Zohran Mamdani's momentum.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ace Frehley, Kiss' original lead guitarist and founding member, dies at 74
item.description: Ace Frehley, the original lead guitarist and founding member of the glam rock band Kiss, who captivated audiences with his elaborate galactic makeup and smoking guitar, died Thursday. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Red Cross sends help from Nevada to Alaska to support flooding evacuees
item.description: A trained volunteer from the American Red Cross of Southern Nevada was sent to Anchorage, Alaska, to help more than 2,000 people displaced by severe flooding.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vatican report calls for reparations for sex abuse victims and tougher sanctions for abusers
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The Vatican’s child protection board said Thursday the Catholic Church has a moral obligation to help victims of clergy sexual abuse heal. Financial reparations for the abused and tougher sanctions for the abusers and their enablers are essential remedies, it said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Food aid is safe this month, but shutdown could put it at risk in November
item.description: A federal program that provides food assistance to 40 million low-income people could be at risk in November if the government shutdown isn't resolved by then.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: GOP, Dems spar over shutdown, violence, ICE arrests in town hall
item.description: NewsNation brought together members of the Trump administration, lawmakers and commentators to address national issues before a live audience.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Smoothie straw leads to arrest in 1984 cold case murder of New York teen
item.description: A straw from a smoothie cup was the evidence that led police in New York to the man they suspect of killing a 16-year-old girl on Long Island in 1984, the Nassau County District Attorney’s Office announced on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, ABC 4

item.title: Smucker sues Trader Joe's over alleged 'dupe' Uncrustables product
item.description: The lawsuit also acknowledged Trader Joe’s use of blue packaging is similar to Smucker’s trademarked branding.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, Fox 5

item.title: Alaska airlifting hundreds from storm-devastated coastal villages
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — One of the most significant airlifts in Alaska history was underway Wednesday to move hundreds of people from coastal villages ravaged by high surf and strong winds from the remnants of Typhoon Halong last weekend, officials said.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hispanic authors and bookstores push for representation in publishing
item.description: Authors, readers and publishing industry experts lament the underrepresentation of Hispanic stories in the mainstream world of books, but have found new ways to elevate the literature and resolve misunderstandings.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP sets the stage for extended shutdown
item.description: Republicans and the Trump administration are bracing for a lengthy government shutdown, taking steps to alleviate any political pain that might boomerang on them while seeking to make life difficult for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: Cinnamon recall expands to 16 brands with elevated lead levels
item.description: The elevated lead levels were discovered during state-level product testing, and the results were then verified by the FDA, according to the agency.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania man pleads guilty in arson attack at governor’s mansion while Shapiro’s family slept
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and used beer bottles filled with gasoline to ignite the occupied Pennsylvania governor’s mansion pleaded guilty Tuesday to the attempted murder of Gov. Josh Shapiro.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Many airports refusing to play Kristi Noem's video that blames Democrats for shutdown
item.description: Multiple airports across the country will not be playing a video that features Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem blaming the government shutdown on Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, Fox 5

item.title: LDS Church announces Dallin H. Oaks as new President
item.description: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints has called President Dallin H. Oaks as the next Prophet of the Church.
item.dc:creator: Renisha Mall, ABC 4

item.title: Shooting at packed South Carolina bar kills 4 and injures at least 20 others
item.description: A mass shooting early Sunday at a crowded bar on an idyllic island considered to be the largest Gullah community on the South Carolina coast has left four people dead and at least 20 injured, officials said.
item.dc:creator: LEWIS LEVINE, Associated Press, Fox 5
